title: Wiki Quality Validator
description: tools/wiki가 위키 품질 계약을 결정론적으로 검증하기 위한 동작 명세.

page.humanSummary: 다음 단계는 위키 품질을 말로 약속하는 것이 아니라 tools/wiki 검증기로 강제하는 것이다.
page.agentSummary: Implement deterministic validation for frontmatter, topology, links, provenance signals, index coverage, log entries, and human-lock safety before extending ingest automation.

body.goal: 현재 품질 게이트 스키마를 실행 가능한 validator와 lint workflow로 고정하여, durable wiki update가 구조적으로 검증되도록 만든다.
body.problem: AGENTS.md와 초기 wiki baseline은 품질 규칙을 정의하지만, 아직 tools/wiki가 이를 강제하지 않는다. 이 상태에서 ingest 자동화를 먼저 만들면 검증되지 않은 LLM 출력이 wiki에 들어갈 수 있다.

in: wiki page discovery and topology checks

architecture.summary: CLI는 thin entrypoint이고, 검증 로직은 focused library modules로 나눈다. Validator는 wiki 파일을 읽고, schema/link/index/log/provenance/human-lock 문제를 issue list로 반환한다. CLI는 이 issue list를 exit code와 report로 노출한다.
flow: 사용자 또는 agent가 python tools/wiki/cli.py validate-page <path> 또는 python tools/wiki/cli.py lint를 실행한다.
CLI가 repository root와 wiki root를 확인한다.
frontmatter parser가 YAML block과 body를 분리한다.
schema validator가 required fields, type/status/confidence/quality enum을 확인한다.
link validator가 [[...]] 링크를 wiki 내 실제 markdown 파일로 해석한다.
index validator가 durable initial/core pages가 index 또는 map에서 도달 가능한지 확인한다.
log validator가 parseable event prefix를 확인한다.
provenance validator가 substantive sections에 Source, Evidence, provenance comment, claim table evidence 중 하나가 있는지 점검한다.

requirements.title: Frontmatter contract validation
requirements.detail: 모든 wiki markdown page는 AGENTS.md Section 4의 required frontmatter와 quality block을 가져야 한다.
acceptanceCriteria: frontmatter가 없으면 validate-page가 실패한다.
required key가 없으면 missing field issue가 출력된다.
quality.provenance, quality.links, quality.contradictions, quality.review_required가 없으면 실패한다.
type, status, confidence, quality enum 값이 허용 목록 밖이면 실패한다.

requirements.title: Topology and durable page discovery
requirements.detail: Validator는 wiki/ 아래 durable markdown page를 대상으로 하고 scratch/의 draft/review 파일을 source truth로 취급하지 않는다.
acceptanceCriteria: wiki/**/*.md 파일은 검증 대상이다.
scratch/drafts와 scratch/review는 durable wiki validation 대상이 아니다.
raw/ 파일은 읽을 수 있지만 수정하거나 wiki page로 오인하지 않는다.

requirements.title: Link validation
requirements.detail: Obsidian-style links must resolve to existing wiki pages where possible.
acceptanceCriteria: [[concepts/compiled-knowledge]]는 wiki/concepts/compiled-knowledge.md로 resolve된다.
존재하지 않는 wiki link는 broken link issue로 보고된다.
plain raw path evidence는 wiki link validation과 분리해서 처리한다.

requirements.title: Index coverage
requirements.detail: 중요 durable pages는 wiki/index.md 또는 map page에서 도달 가능해야 한다.
acceptanceCriteria: overview, inbox, log, initial concept/workflow/decision/map pages가 index에 없으면 lint가 실패한다.
새 durable page가 index와 map 어디에도 없으면 orphan/navigation issue로 보고된다.
index에 존재하지 않는 page link가 있으면 broken index issue로 보고된다.

requirements.title: Log event validation
requirements.detail: wiki/log.md entries must keep the parseable prefix required by AGENTS.md.
acceptanceCriteria: ## [YYYY-MM-DD] event-type | Short Title 형식이 하나 이상 있어야 한다.
event-type이 허용 목록 밖이면 issue로 보고한다.
append-only 여부는 이 단계에서 Git diff 기반으로 강제하지 않고 future work로 둔다.

requirements.title: Provenance signal validation
requirements.detail: Validator는 claim-level truth를 판정하지 않지만, section-level provenance signal 누락은 탐지해야 한다.
acceptanceCriteria: quality.provenance가 section 또는 claim인데 본문에 Source, Evidence, provenance comment, Supporting Sources, claim table evidence가 전혀 없으면 issue를 낸다.
quality.provenance가 none인 page는 source_count가 0이어야 한다.
source_count는 증거가 아니라 lintable metadata로 취급한다.

requirements.title: Human-lock safety
requirements.detail: human-locked pages and sections must be detected so future write paths can avoid unsafe overwrites.
acceptanceCriteria: <!-- human-locked --> marker is reported as locked page metadata.
<!-- human-locked:start --> and <!-- human-locked:end --> ranges are detected.
unmatched lock markers produce a validation issue.

requirements.title: Report and exit code contract
requirements.detail: CLI output must be actionable for humans and agents.
acceptanceCriteria: No issues returns exit code 0.
Validation issues return non-zero exit code.
lint can write scratch/reports/YYYY-MM-DD-lint.md.
Report groups issues by severity and file path.

approaches.name: Minimal deterministic Python CLI
tradeoffs: Fastest path with no external runtime dependency beyond Python standard library plus optional PyYAML.
Good fit for current repo size and local-first workflow.
YAML parsing requires either a tiny constrained parser or dependency decision.
approaches.recommendation: Recommended for the next step. Keep the first validator local and deterministic before adding MCP.

approaches.name: Full MCP-first implementation
tradeoffs: Premature before CLI contract is stable.
Can hide local validation semantics behind protocol details.

approaches.name: Markdown-only manual checklist
tradeoffs: Does not enforce quality and repeats the root problem.
Hard for future agents to verify consistently.

components.name: tools/wiki/cli.py
components.responsibility: Expose validate-page and lint commands with stable exit codes.

components.name: tools/wiki/lib/frontmatter.py
components.responsibility: Parse YAML frontmatter and markdown body without mutating files.
dependencies: Python standard library or explicitly chosen YAML parser

components.name: tools/wiki/lib/schema.py
components.responsibility: Validate required frontmatter fields and enum values.

components.name: tools/wiki/lib/links.py
components.responsibility: Extract and resolve Obsidian-style links against wiki markdown files.

components.name: tools/wiki/lib/lint.py
components.responsibility: Coordinate repo-wide checks and report generation.

dataFlow: Markdown files are read from wiki/.
Each file is parsed into frontmatter and body.
Validators emit structured issues with severity, path, message, and optional line.
CLI prints a concise summary and optionally writes a report.
Tests assert both passing baseline behavior and failing fixture behavior.

errorHandling.behavior: Return an issue tied to the file and exit non-zero for validate-page.
Return a broken-link issue without editing the page.
Return an error issue and continue linting other files when running repo-wide lint.
Report needs-review; do not rewrite or judge the claim.

contracts.name: Durable wiki boundary
rules: Only wiki/**/*.md pages are durable compiled knowledge.
scratch/drafts and scratch/review are not source truth.
raw/ must never be modified by validator commands.

contracts.name: Quality metadata boundary
rules: Frontmatter stores routing and quality triage metadata.
Claim evidence belongs in the body near the claim.
Validation reports belong in scratch/reports, not as churn-heavy timestamps in every page.

surfaces.role: CLI entrypoint for validation and linting.

testingStrategy.expected: New validator tests cover missing frontmatter, broken links, bad enum values, log format, and provenance signal checks.

validation.purpose: Confirm rendered Stenc pages exist for JSON sources.

agentInstructions: Read this spec before implementing tools/wiki validation.
Do not implement MCP until validate-page and lint have stable local behavior.
Use tests that demonstrate the validator fails on malformed wiki pages before making it pass.
Do not let CLI write to raw/.
Do not use frontmatter as a replacement for claim-level evidence in the markdown body.
reviewChecklist: Every requirement maps to a plan slice.
The first implementation target is deterministic validation, not ingest or MCP.
The spec preserves the durable boundary between raw, scratch, and wiki.
The spec does not require LLM judgment inside deterministic tooling.

selfReviewChecks.name: Root-cause alignment
selfReviewChecks.purpose: Confirm the spec solves the enforcement gap instead of adding another checklist.

selfReviewChecks.name: Boundary clarity
selfReviewChecks.purpose: Confirm raw, scratch, wiki, and tools responsibilities are distinct.

selfReviewChecks.name: Testability
selfReviewChecks.purpose: Confirm every required behavior can be tested locally.

notes: Proceed only after this spec and its related plan validate and render through Stenc.

supportingSections.heading: Quality Position
supportingSections.content: The wiki quality system should make invalid states visible and block completion claims until validation passes.
items: tools/wiki validation is the write boundary.

openQuestions: Should the first parser depend on PyYAML, or should it support only the constrained YAML shapes used in this vault?
